title: LangGraph- Develop LLM powered AI agents with LangGraph
headline: Learn LangGraph by building FAST a real world LLM AI Agents (Python)

instructor.name: Eden Marco
instructor.title: GenAI Architect @ Google Cloud, LangChain Ambassador
instructor.bio: Eden Marco is a GenAI Architect at Google Cloud, helping organizations design and deploy advanced generative AI and cloud-native solutions. He is a LangChain Ambassador actively contributing to the open-source community.

description: This comprehensive course teaches developers how to leverage the LangGraph library for building sophisticated LLM-powered agentic applications. The instructor positions LangGraph as superior to standard LangChain for complex agent flows, emphasizing 'flow engineering' as a strategic approach to AI system development. This is NOT a beginner course.
what_you_will_learn: Gain proficiency with LangGraph for building LLM agentic applications
Implement advanced agent patterns (Multi-agent, Reflection, Reflexion)
Build end-to-end working generative AI agents
Navigate the LangGraph open-source codebase
Master the LangGraph ecosystem (Studio/IDE, Cloud API, Cloud Managed Service)
Advanced RAG architectures: Corrective RAG (CRAG), Adaptive RAG, Self-RAG
Parallel execution with fan-out/fan-in patterns
Human-in-the-loop workflows
Persistence and checkpointing
CrewAI vs LangGraph comparison
prerequisites: Strong Python proficiency (environment variables, package management, OOP, Git)
curriculum_sections: Introduction & Fundamentals (LangGraph overview, graph theory, state machines, flow engineering)
Core Components (Nodes, edges, conditional edges, state management, node functions)
Advanced Agent Patterns (Multi-agent systems, Reflection agents, Reflexion agents)
RAG Architectures (Corrective RAG, Adaptive RAG, Self-RAG, Advanced RAG)
Production Deployment (LangGraph Studio/IDE, Docker, LangGraph Cloud API, persistent state)
Advanced Features (Parallel execution, Human-in-the-loop, Persistence, CrewAI vs LangGraph)

reviews.quote: Incredibly well-structured and comprehensive dive into LangGraph
Beautiful course. Like the training and I've just completed the LangChain am much better for it
Covers wide array of advanced agent patterns and RAG architectures
Eden has been doing a commendable job at trying to keep up, frequently modifying/updating the course content
Struggled with this course without advanced LangChain knowledge - extremely rapid pace assumes very high existing expertise

title: LangChain- Agentic AI Engineering with LangChain & LangGraph
headline: Build AI Agents with LangChain and LangGraph RAG, Tools, MCP and Production-Ready Agentic AI Systems (Python)

instructor.name: Eden Marco
instructor.title: GenAI Architect @ Google Cloud, LangChain Ambassador
instructor.bio: Eden Marco is a GenAI Architect at Google Cloud, helping organizations design and deploy advanced generative AI and cloud-native solutions. He is a LangChain Ambassador actively contributing to the open-source community.

description: Re-recorded in 2026 supporting LangChain Version 1.2+. The course teaches how to design and build AI agents and agentic AI systems using LangChain and LangGraph, covering production-ready AI agents, RAG systems, and advanced LLM applications. This is NOT a beginner course.
what_you_will_learn: Design and build AI agents and agentic AI systems using LangChain and LangGraph
Production-ready AI agents, RAG systems, and advanced LLM applications
Prompt Engineering Theory: Chain of Thought, ReAct, Few Shot prompting
Understand how LangChain is built under the hood
Output Parsers, Chains (SequentialChain, LLMChain, RetrievalQA chain)
Agents and Custom Agents, Tools and Toolkits
Memory management and Vectorstores (Pinecone, FAISS)
DocumentLoaders and TextSplitters
MCP - Model Context Protocol with LangChain
Build a Documentation Helper chatbot (RAG over Python package docs)
prerequisites: Familiar with git, Python, pipenv, environment variables, classes, testing and debugging
curriculum_sections: Prompt Engineering Theory (Chain of Thought, ReAct, Few Shot)
LangChain Core (Output Parsers, Chains, Tools, Memory, Vectorstores)
[Optional] Ice Breaker Real World Generative AI Agent Application
Let's Talk About LLM Applications In Production
Intro to MCP - Model Context Protocol with LangChain
Useful Tools When Developing LLM Applications

reviews.quote: Hands-down the most practical and up-to-date LangChain course after trying 4 different ones
Eden Marco clearly uses LangChain in production, and it shows in how he structures the course. He doesn't just show you HOW to use LangChain — he explains WHEN and WHY to use each component
Covers LangChain 0.3.0+ (the latest version — many courses are stuck on 0.1.x)
The prompt engineering coverage was worth the price alone
Great course that takes you from zero to hero in building AI agents with LangChain and LangGraph

title: The Complete LangChain, LangGraph, & LangSmith Course (2026)
headline: Modularize with LangChain, Build Agents with LangGraph, and Monitor with LangSmith

instructor.name: Fikayo Adepoju
instructor.title: Serial Author with 10+ years building distributed apps
instructor.bio: Fikayo Adepoju is a Full-stack developer, technical writer and tech content creator proficient in Web and Mobile technologies and DevOps with over 10 years experience developing scalable distributed applications. He has written over 40 articles for Twilio, CircleCI, Auth0 and The New Stack blogs.
instructor.other_courses: The Complete HTMX Course (4.2 rating, 167 reviews)

total_hours_note: ~29h 30m (API reported 106266 seconds of video content). Note: external sources cite 80 lectures / 25h 46m which may reflect an earlier version.
description: The most complete, up-to-date guide on the modern AI stack: LangChain, LangGraph, and LangSmith. Designed to take you from a beginner to a professional AI Engineer by mastering the three pillars of the LangChain ecosystem. Published January 2026.
what_you_will_learn: Master LCEL Orchestration: Build highly efficient, composable AI pipelines using the LangChain Expression Language and Runnable interfaces
Develop Autonomous Agents: Use create_agent to build intelligent assistants that can reason and interact with external tools
Architect Stateful Graphs: Design non-linear AI workflows using LangGraph to handle complex branching and looping logic
Implement Robust Persistence: Use Checkpointers to save graph state, enabling agents to resume tasks and maintain memory
Enable Production Monitoring: Set up LangSmith to trace every step of your application's execution for rapid debugging and optimization
Evaluate AI Performance: Use LangSmith datasets and evaluation functions to measure and improve AI system quality
prerequisites: Basic Python Knowledge (familiarity with Python syntax, functions, and list comprehensions)
Understanding of API basics and API key usage
Problem-solving mindset (prior AI experience not required)
curriculum_sections: LangChain Fundamentals (LCEL, Runnable Chains, efficient async pipelines)
LangGraph (Agentic workflows, cyclic state machines, branching and looping logic)
Persistence (Checkpointers for saving graph state, resuming tasks)
LangSmith (Production monitoring, tracing, debugging complex reasoning paths)
Evaluation (LangSmith datasets and evaluation suites for AI performance)

reviews.quote: Highest rated LangGraph course on Udemy for 3 months in a row
Explanations were super clear and easy to follow (referencing instructor's teaching style)
reviews.source: Student review of instructor's other course

title: LangGraph for beginners : Agentic Workflows in simple steps
headline: Learn to Build Intelligent Agents, One Step at a Time

instructor.name: Bharath Thippireddy
instructor.title: IT Architect and Best Selling Instructor
instructor.bio: Bharath Thippireddy is an Entrepreneur, Software Architect, Actor and Public Speaker who has trained 850,000+ students across the planet. He is an Oracle Certified Developer, Web Component Developer, Business Component Developer, and Web Services Developer.

description: A beginner-friendly course that teaches LangGraph, an open-source library built on top of LangChain, designed for orchestrating multi-agent applications using a graph-based architecture. Originally published April 2025.
what_you_will_learn: What LangGraph is and how it fits into the GenAI ecosystem
Build your first LangGraph workflow using a state machine
Validate and structure state using Pydantic models
Use async and streaming to build responsive applications
Implement conditional routing based on LLM output
Understand reducers and how they manage state transitions
Master tool calling with LangGraph's built-in ToolNode
Checkpointers: short-term (in-memory) and long-term (SQLite, Redis) memory storage
Build Agentic RAG workflows using tools and retrievers
Implement Human-in-the-Loop workflows using Interrupt and resume
Modularize complex graphs using subgraphs
Real-world business use cases: claim processing, customer support, document analysis
curriculum_sections: Real-World Business Use Cases (Claim Processing, Customer Support, Document Analysis)

title: Master LangGraph v1 and Ollama - Build Gen AI Agents
headline: Gen AI, LangChain v1, MCP, MySQL, DeepSeek, GPT-OSS, Qwen3, LLAMA, LangGraph, Ollama, KGPTalkie

instructor.name: Laxmi Kant (KGP Talkie)
instructor.title: Senior Data Science & AI Engineer, IIT Kharagpur Graduate
instructor.bio: Laxmi Kant Tiwari is an IIT Kharagpur graduate and a Senior Data Science & AI Engineer with 10+ years of industry experience across startups and MNCs. Through KGP Talkie, his global teaching platform, Laxmi has trained 100,000+ students worldwide in Python, Machine Learning, Deep Learning, and cutting-edge Generative AI technologies. Many of his courses have become Best Sellers on Udemy.

total_hours_note: ~13h 42m (API reported 49383 seconds of video content). Note: TutorialsPoint lists 76 lectures / 8 hours for an earlier version; current version was re-designed and re-recorded for 2026.
description: A complete and beginner-friendly course re-designed, re-recorded, and rebuilt from the ground up to support LangChain v1+, LangGraph v1+, latest frameworks, and modern agentic workflows. Build real AI agents using LangGraph, LangChain, Ollama, and open source LLMs like GPT-OSS, Qwen3 and Gemma3.
what_you_will_learn: Install and integrate Ollama with LangChain v1
Run models like Qwen3, Gemma3, GPT-OSS, DeepSeek-R1, and build custom GGUF models
LangGraph v1 from scratch: states, nodes, reducers, nested nodes, conditional routing, full graph execution
Build complete AI agents with ReAct, tool calling, memory saver, streaming
Long-term memory, short-term memory, and agent state management
Design and deploy sentiment analyzers, tweet handlers, weather tools, calculator tools
Multi-step agent workflows
Implement agentic memory systems with SQLite + PostgreSQL persistence
Semantic memory search and retrieval pipelines
Create guardrails, interrupts, and human-in-the-loop approvals
Build advanced agents: Reflection Agent, Critique Agent, Research Agent, Model-Selection Agent
MCP (Model Context Protocol) integration
MySQL database integration for intelligent query execution
Agentic RAG for private datasets
curriculum_sections: Ollama Setup for Local LLM (model installation, benchmarking, uncensored models)
Getting Started with LangChain (API integration, LangSmith setup)
Agentic Text to MySQL Query Execution
AI Agents with ReAct and Tool Calling
